output.final_deliverable:
I was asked to visit the Wikipedia main page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and collect 10 links with URL, link text/title, and a 1–2 sentence description for each. However, no browsing/visit results were provided in the merged results (the "merged" array is empty), and I have no evidence from a web visit to extract those links and descriptions. According to the instructions, I must use only evidence present in the merged results; because those results are missing, I cannot produce the requested list of 10 links with verified URLs and descriptions.

What is missing:
1) A web visit or search result containing the content of https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page or extraction results from that page. Without that evidence I cannot list exact link URLs, link text, or verified descriptions.

How I can proceed (choose one):
- Option A: I will fetch the Wikipedia main page now and extract 10 links with URL, link text/title, and 1–2 sentence descriptions, and then return the structured list. (I will include explicit URLs and short descriptions and cite the visited page URL.)
- Option B: If you prefer to supply the page content or a set of links from the main page, I can extract and format the required information from that content.

Please tell me which option you want, and I will continue. If you choose Option A, I will visit https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page, collect 10 links, and return them in the requested structured format with full URLs and brief descriptions.
